title: penstemon campanulatus subsp. subglandulosus
intro: Penstemon campanulatus subsp. subglandulosus, a lesser-known subspecies of the penstemon genus, is a flowering plant native to the mountainous regions of the western United States. It has been used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, which include antibacterial and anti-inflammatory effects. The health benefits of this plant include its potential to treat respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma, due to its expectorant properties, as well as its ability to soothe digestive issues and alleviate menstrual cramps. Penstemon campanulatus subsp. subglandulosus can be prepared in various herbal forms, including teas, tinctures, and infusions, allowing for a range of applications in traditional medicine.
